summary: Heavily obfuscated bulk email/SMS sender with offensive 'red-team' tooling
details: Package self-describes as an 'Advanced email sender' with 'red-team' and 'smtp' keywords. The tarball ships ~40 files under.ad/ that are all RC4+base64 string-array obfuscated. The CLI exposes bulk SMS sending, bulk email sending, MX-provider sorting, DKIM key generation, OAuth-based contact harvesting from the user's own Microsoft/Google accounts, and redirect-probe scanners — a feature set characteristic of spam/phishing infrastructure. Execution is gated by the author's remote license server. The main entry (.ad/x0.js) is also the bin, and ends with program.parse(), so requiring the package as a library will run the CLI parser against the consumer's process.argv (no network I/O triggers without an explicit subcommand). The postinstall script only creates a Windows shim. There is no install-time or require-time exfiltration of installer data, no remote code fetch-and-execute, no credential scraping of installer secrets, and no destruction of the installer environment. The harm pattern (bulk spam/phishing) is directed at third parties the operator chooses to target, not at the developer who installs the package — so it does not satisfy this system's installer-harm threshold for an automatic block. The combination of heavy obfuscation across every shipped file and an offensive declared purpose warrants human review for policy reasons (registry ToS, abuse).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
